sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports fatigue for the past two months. He occasionally experiences numbness and tingling in his feet, particularly in cold weather. He denies fever or chills. He reports a history of smoking, having stopped approximately four years ago after smoking at least two packs per day for twenty plus years. He reports no pain in his foot.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Vital signs reveal a blood pressure of 127/78 and a heart rate of 68. A two out of six systolic ejection murmur is auscultated at the left base. A two by three centimeter ulcerated lesion is observed on the right lateral foot near the fifth metatarsal-metatarsophalangeal joint, with associated granulation tissue and slight purulent discharge. Neurological examination is nonfocal.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient has a non-healing ulcer on his right foot, likely related to his diabetes and vascular risk. The ulceration is concerning for potential infection and requires further evaluation.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: Order an arterial ultrasound of the lower extremities to assess vascular supply. Arrange a podiatry consultation for wound care management. Continue aspirin therapy. Schedule a discussion with the primary care physician regarding diabetes management and optimization. Continue statin therapy as prescribed.
medical_concepts: arterial ultrasound of the lower extremities
arterial ultrasound of the lower extremities
